heading: Agency use of amounts for property management
label: 124 Agency use of amounts for property management
text: Amounts appropriated, allocated, or available to a federal agency for purposes similar to the purposes in or subchapter I (except section 506), II, or III of chapter 5 of this title may be used by the agency for the disposition of property under this subtitle, and for the care and handling of property pending the disposition, if the Director of the Office of Management and Budget authorizes the use.section 121 of this title
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)12440:475(b)., title VI, § 603(b), formerly § 503(b), ; renumbered [§] 603(b), , (b), .June 30, 1949, ch. 28863 Stat. 403Sept. 5, 1950, ch. 849, § 6(a)64 Stat. 583The words “heretofore or hereafter” are omitted as unnecessary. The words “Director of the Office of Management and Budget” are substituted for “Director of the Bureau of the Budget” in section 603(b) of the Federal Property and Administrative Services Act of 1949 because the office of Director of the Bureau of the Budget was redesignated the Director of the Office of Management and Budget by section 102(b) of Reorganization Plan No. 2 of 1970 (). Section 102 of Reorganization Plan No. 2 of 1970, was repealed by (, ), the first section of which enacted Title 31, United States Code, but the successor provision, 31:502, continued the designation as Director of the Office of Management and Budget.84 Stat. 2085section 5(b) of the Act of September 13, 1982Public Law 97–25896 Stat. 1085
